reason: The fix involves a simple configuration edit to /etc/security/pwquality.conf to enforce lowercase character requirements. This is a standard PAM password policy hardening change that does not affect system boot, networking, or filesystem structures and poses minimal risk of lockout when applied reasonably.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Overly strict password policies could lock out users if existing passwords do not meet new requirements; Misconfiguration of pam_pwquality could disable password quality checking
